summary: Eight hand-rolled `Command::new(BIN)` `temper check` runners survive the consolidation that exists to leave one ‚Äî four in the consolidated file itself, four in three suites never in its scope

edit.description: Four `temper check` runs still hand-roll `Command::new(BIN)` in the very file 3bd0f80 consolidated: 210 (`check <ws> --harness <harness>`, status only), 248 (`check <harness>` from the ambient cwd), 267 (bare `check` from the harness), 286 (`self_host_check_is_clean_over_tempers_own_surface`, bare `check` from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`). Each is `common::check_in(cwd, args, reporter)` ‚Äî its first parameter IS the cwd, so 248's deliberate cwd-not-root spelling survives the fold at full strength (pass the crate root as cwd, the harness as the positional arg). The file already `mod common`s (19). `const BIN` (22) SURVIVES ‚Äî `schema`, `guard`, `--help`, and the retired-verb loop still read it; do not predict its death (3bd0f80 made exactly that miss on `requirement_roster`'s `BIN`, which `explain_in` still reads).

`check_harness_and_session_start_gate_the_raw_harness_with_no_scratch_import` (1473) hand-rolls two check runners: 1483 (`check --harness <harness>`) and 1495 (`check <harness> --reporter session-start`). Both fold onto `common::check_in`; `mod common` is already at 32. `const BIN` (35) SURVIVES ‚Äî the two `emit` runners at 1422/1451 read it, and `emit` is not `check_in`'s job.

172 hand-rolls `check . --reporter sarif` from the harness cwd ‚Äî `common::check_in(&harness, &["."], Some("sarif"))`. `mod common` at 18. This is `BIN`'s ONLY use in the file (24), so `const BIN` retires with the fold ‚Äî `rg BIN tests/reporters.rs` finds those two lines and nothing else.

`run_session_start` (48) hand-rolls `check <harness> --reporter session-start` and parses stdout as JSON ‚Äî folds onto `common::check_in`, whose `CheckRun.stdout` field (added by 3bd0f80 for exactly this stream-specific need) serves the parse. `mod common` at 18. The honest exception, named not papered: 360 drives `temper::install::SESSION_START_COMMAND`'s own token stream (`.split_whitespace().skip(1)`) ‚Äî its subject is that the wired command string works, so `check` arrives as data inside `args`, not as the verb a runner prepends. `check_in` prepends `check` and cannot express it; leave it hand-rolled, and `const BIN` (24) SURVIVES for it. The `.temper/kinds/` + `.temper/packages/` fixtures at 121/140 are NOT this entry's to touch ‚Äî see notes.

`check_in`'s doc (155-158) currently homes only the GitHub-reporter harness shape, at [`check_harness`]. Its predecessor's call-site-adaptation invitation is what regrew six copies (3bd0f80's own root-cause finding), so the narrower re-spelling invites the same regrowth: re-word it to state that a `check` run goes through this home whatever its reporter, arg shape, or cwd ‚Äî the one shape it does not serve being a caller driving a command string that carries the verb itself. No new helper: `check_in` already takes cwd, args, and reporter, so every folded caller becomes a call, not a wrapper.

tests.asserts: unchanged behavior ‚Äî the existing assertions across the four suites are the test; 248's explicit-root-vs-bare-verb pair keeps its cwd distinction, and 170's stdout-specific assertion keeps reading `CheckRun.stdout`

acceptance: Exactly one hand-rolled `temper check` runner survives in `tests/` ‚Äî `session_start.rs:360`, driving `SESSION_START_COMMAND` ‚Äî and every other `check` run reaches the binary through `common::check_in` (engineering.md, 'One job, one home': a job the tree already performs is extended where it lives; shared test scaffolding lives in one home, never copy-pasted per file). `tests/reporters.rs` no longer declares `BIN`. `cargo test` green (679 passing), `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
notes: The direct remainder of CHECK-HARNESS-ONE-HOME (3bd0f80), which scoped 'nine suites': `cli.rs`'s other four runs were outside its named scope, and `emit`/`reporters`/`session_start` were never in it. Its body claims 'every caller reaches it' ‚Äî true of the six copies and the two named runners, not of these eight. No behavior moves. `session_start.rs`'s retired-format fixtures are a separate class this entry must not fold in (state.md carries why). scoped at 3593ab6

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics ‚Äî the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (‚áí 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (‚áí 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence ‚Äî never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at 3593ab6. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' ‚Äî a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts ‚Äî hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it ‚Äî 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') ‚Äî carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at 3593ab6) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment ‚Äî `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding ‚Äî so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper ‚Äî the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 3593ab6 and holds ‚Äî nothing ruled the hop semantics; inbox empty and the reconciled window is one test-consolidation commit touching no import mechanics: `git diff d88ed75..3593ab6 -- src/graph.rs tests/graph.rs` is empty. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` still reads 5 at 65 under a cite claiming five, and 525/624/654 plus `tests/graph.rs:1356` are unmoved. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified 3593ab6.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary‚Üînpm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) ‚Äî no signing creds ‚Äî but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 3593ab6, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. The reconciled window is test-only and touched no release surface. scoped at b676656.
